mnemonics: Silly Pandas Stop Nibbling Apples (for Stop-and-Wait, Go-Back-N ARQ, Selective Repeat ARQ)
Sliding Windows Slide Data (to remember the concept of a sliding window protocol)
keyPoints: Flow control manages the rate of data transmission to prevent a fast sender from overwhelming a slow receiver.
Error control uses techniques like Automatic Repeat Request (ARQ) to ensure reliable data delivery.
The main protocols are Stop-and-Wait ARQ, Go-Back-N ARQ, and Selective Repeat ARQ, each with different efficiency and complexity.
commonMistakes: Confusing flow control (receiver-focused) with congestion control (network-focused).

Assuming Go-Back-N and Selective Repeat handle out-of-order packets the same way; Go-Back-N re-transmits all packets from the lost one, while Selective Repeat only re-transmits the specific lost packet.
